title: Yin yang fried rice
summary:
Yin yang fried rice (also transliterated as yuenyeung fried rice or yuanyang fried rice; Chinese: йґ›йґ¦з‚’йЈЇ; pinyin: yuДЃnyДЃng chЗЋofГ n; Jyutping: jyun1 joeng1 caau2 faan6) is a rice dish from Hong Kong, consisting of a plate of rice with bГ©chamel sauce and tomato sauce.
The name "yuenyeung" refers to mandarin ducks вЂ” a symbol of conjugal love in Chinese culture вЂ” as the birds usually appear in pairs and are highly sexually dimorphic in their appearance. The dish is named after this same connotation of a compatible "pair" of two dissimilar items: the bГ©chamel and tomato sauces. Due to the romantic association, this dish is often served in Hong Kong wedding dinners.

The name "yuenyeung" refers to mandarin ducks вЂ” a symbol of conjugal love in Chinese culture вЂ” as the birds usually appear in pairs and are highly sexually dimorphic in their appearance. The dish is named after this same connotation of a compatible "pair" of two dissimilar items: the bГ©chamel and tomato sauces.
